pattern: Pairing an array (for uniform random indexing) with a hash map of positions (for constant-time lookup) — and filling deletion holes with the last element — gives every operation true O(1).
hints: A set alone gives O(1) insert and remove but no way to pick uniformly at random; an array alone makes removal linear.
Store values in an array and remember each value's index in a map.
To remove, swap the victim with the last array element, pop, and fix the moved element's index — getRandom is then just a random array slot.

edge_inputs.description: insert, duplicate insert, remove, random over survivors
remove from empty then insert into the hole
duplicate inserts must report false without corrupting state
getRandom over a single element is fully determined
remove the most recent insert repeatedly (swap-with-last edge)

stress.description: random op mix that only asks getRandom when the set is non-empty
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    ops = ['RandomizedSet']
    args = [[]]
    present = set()
    pool = list(range(-20, 21))
    for _ in range(max(1, min(size, 400))):
        r = rng.random()
        if r < 0.45 or not present:
            v = rng.choice(pool)
            ops.append('insert')
            args.append([v])
            present.add(v)
        elif r < 0.75:
            v = rng.choice(pool)
            ops.append('remove')
            args.append([v])
            present.discard(v)
        else:
            ops.append('getRandom')
            args.append([])
    return [ops, args]
